'rabbana la tu'akhidhna in-nasina aw akhta'na'. The difference between Error and Forgetfulness and the Wisdom of this Prayer: The preceding sentence was merely for the consolation of the believers and a timely reminder of reality. Now the original prayer flowed from the tongue again. In this segment, the request is made not to be held accountable for forgetfulness and error. 'Forgetfulness' (Nisyan) is for a person to forget something while fulfilling the responsibility of hearing and obeying, and 'Error' (Khata') is to do a task wrongly out of their own lack of understanding. Although these things are forgiven by Allah Almighty, the request for forgiveness for forgiven things is an expression of the highest degree of awe (khashiyat) from the servant, which opens further doors of Allah Almighty's mercy and favor.

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) had all his previous and future sins forgiven, yet he used to endure the maximum hardship in asking for forgiveness (istighfar). When he was asked the reason for this, he said: "Should I not desire to be a grateful servant of my Lord?"

'rabbana wa la tahmil 'alayna isran kama hamaltahu 'alal-ladhina min qablina'. The Meaning of 'Isr' and the Secret of this Prayer: The original meaning of 'Isr' is a covenant and responsibility. For example: 'Do you acknowledge and take upon that My Isr [responsibility/covenant]?' (Al Imran: 81). From here, its usage became for those heavy and weighty responsibilities and burdens whose carrying is difficult. In this meaning, this word is used here. In the Sharia of the Jews, such 'Isr' and 'Aghlal' (shackles)—the details of which will come in the Tafseer of Surah Al-A'raf—existed. And among the objectives of the advent of the Seal of the Prophets, Muhammad the Messenger of Allah (PBUH), was this objective: that you would liberate Allah's Sharia from those heavy collars and chains and establish it on the foundation of nature (fitrah), as stated: 'And he relieves them of their burden [israhum] and the shackles which were upon them' (Al-A'raf: 157). Here in these words of the prayer, the hint is toward this very point: that such types of burdens should not be placed upon us as were placed upon the Jews due to their rebellion and which they ultimately could not carry. These words of the prayer show that there is perfect compatibility between the temperament of this Ummah and the temperament of the Sharia it received.

'rabbana wa la tuhammilna ma la taqata lana bih'. Prayer for Protection from Trials beyond Strength: 'Tahmil' means to load a heavy burden upon someone. The meaning is: among the trials that are to be encountered ahead in this path of hearing and obeying, let there be no trial that is beyond our endurance and which makes us fail in the test of Your loyalty. As far as 'Ibtila' (trial) and testing are concerned, they are a requirement of faith and Islam—in fact, a requirement of life—from which there is no escape in this world. To distinguish the genuine from the fake and to highlight the capabilities of servants, passing through it is necessary. But the servant should keep making this prayer: that no test be beyond their strength. Acknowledging one's own weakness and powerlessness regarding Allah Almighty's trial is the correct attitude; those who place too much confidence in themselves often fail in that trial. Prohibition of this has come from various angles in the Hadiths.

Here, the difference between the preceding segment and this segment should also be noted. In the preceding segment, the prayer is that our Sharia remains pure from the type of 'Isr' and 'Aghlal' present in previous Sharias. And in this second segment, the prayer is for protection from those trials beyond capacity which might be encountered in the path of fulfilling the rights of this Sharia.

'wa'fu 'anna waghfir lana warhamna'. In this, three things are requested together: 'Afw' (pardon), 'Maghfirat' (forgiveness), and 'Rahm' (mercy). 'Afw' means turning a blind eye as well as pardoning. Here the word is in the second meaning. 'Ghafr' means to cover/veil. The meaning of 'Rahm' is clear. The servant's entire reliance should be only on these three things. That the Kind Lord overlooks shortcomings, covers sins, and blesses with His mercy—the entire support of the Hereafter is just these three things.

'anta mawlana fansurna 'alal-qawmil-kafirin'. 'Mawla' means the place of return to whom one turns in difficulties. This is finally a prayer for help and victory against the opponents of Islam. This is because the Ummah has carried this heavy burden of hearing and obeying in circumstances when others—as has become clear from previous details—had not only thrown this burden from their shoulders but had also become the mortal enemies of the Muslims because they [the Muslims] took up this burden which they [the others] had thrown away.

These are the final lines which this sinful and worthless one was blessed with the success to write in the Tafseer of this Surah. And our final call is that all praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.
